meta.title: Goal-Setting Test
meta.description: A 500-question reflection across 20 dimensions of effective goal-setting.

levels.label: Strategic Goal-Setter
levels.desc: You consistently translate meaningful aims into clear, realistic, trackable action and learn from progress.

levels.label: Purposeful Planner
levels.desc: Your goal-setting habits are strong across most areas, with a few dimensions that could become more deliberate.

levels.label: Developing Strategist
levels.desc: You use several effective goal-setting practices, though planning or follow-through may vary by situation.

levels.label: Emerging Planner
levels.desc: Your intentions are present, but clearer structure, tracking, and obstacle planning could improve follow-through.

levels.label: Inconsistent Goal Process
levels.desc: Goals may often remain vague, unplanned, or difficult to sustain. Focus on one or two foundational habits first.

levels.label: Goal Foundations Needed
levels.desc: Your current approach may rely more on wishes than a repeatable process. Small, specific next steps are a useful starting point.

questions.text: When choosing a new personal goal, how likely are you to state clearly what you want to achieve?
When planning a school or work project, how likely are you to leave the desired result vague?
At the beginning of a new month, how likely are you to describe the desired end result in plain language?
When a goal initially feels exciting, how likely are you to begin without deciding what success should look like?
After deciding that something should change, how likely are you to state clearly what you want to achieve?
When several goals compete for attention, how likely are you to leave the desired result vague?
